post_topic: CI/CD failure paths — why the happy path is a lie and what to actually diagram
image_description:
Single standalone diagram. One concept only. No panels, no grids, no collages. Full bleed 1080x1080px white background.

A left-to-right GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline flow diagram titled 'CI/CD Failure Path Map' in bold navy text at the top.

The happy path runs as a horizontal spine across the center of the diagram with five sequential nodes connected by rightward arrows: 'Code Push (PR Merge)' → 'Lint & Validate' → 'Terraform Plan' → 'HITL Approval Gate' → 'Terraform Apply' → 'Deployed ✓'. All spine nodes are navy rounded rectangles with white text.

Below or branching off each spine node, amber-colored failure exit paths split downward:

1. Below 'Lint & Validate': amber diamond labeled 'Lint Fails?' with a downward arrow to an amber box 'Block PR — no deploy'. A small label on the branch reads 'exit: hard stop'.

2. Below 'Terraform Plan': amber diamond 'Plan Errors?' with arrow to amber box 'Notify + Cancel Job'. Label: 'exit: clean cancel'.

3. Below 'HITL Approval Gate': amber diamond 'Timeout / No Response?' with arrow to amber box 'Job Cancels — Lock Released?' with a red question mark callout next to it labeled 'Dangling lock risk'. Label: 'exit: undefined behavior'.

4. Below 'Terraform Apply': amber diamond 'Apply Fails Mid-Run?' branching into two amber boxes side by side — 'State File Updated?' and 'Environment Half-Configured?' — connected with a dashed line between them and a red callout label 'Partial failure zone'.

5. A separate amber branch between 'Code Push' and 'Lint & Validate': a small amber box labeled 'Queue Collision (2 PRs)' with an arrow pointing to 'Race condition — undefined apply order'.

At the far right of the diagram, a blue summary box with white text lists: 'What failure paths expose: 1. Gaps in explicit error handling  2. HITL trigger points  3. Observability blind spots'.

GitHub Actions logo appears near 'Code Push' node. Terraform logo appears near 'Terraform Plan' and 'Terraform Apply' nodes.

Navy #0A0F1E primary elements, blue #5a96ff highlights, amber #F5A623 callouts and important labels. Clean sans-serif font. Real cloud provider logos where relevant. Small text "Kelvin Davis" bottom right corner. Professional LinkedIn infographic style similar to ByteByteGo. White background. No dark backgrounds.
